categories.name: Cleaning
categories.description: A clean home is a happy home

categories.name: Safety
categories.description: Keep your family safe at home

tips.title: Change a Light Bulb
tips.subtitle: Swap out old bulbs in just a few minutes.

steps.title: Turn off the switch
steps.description: Always switch off the light before touching the bulb. Wait a minute if it was on — it might be hot.

steps.title: Remove the old bulb
steps.description: Gently twist the bulb to the left (counterclockwise) until it comes out. Hold it firmly but don't squeeze.

steps.title: Put in the new bulb
steps.description: Take your new bulb and twist it to the right (clockwise) until it feels snug. Don't over-tighten.

steps.title: Turn the switch back on
steps.description: Flip the switch. Your new bulb should light up right away!

tips.proTip: LED bulbs last much longer and use less power. They're worth the extra cost.

tips.title: Understand Your Distribution Board
tips.subtitle: Know what each switch in that metal box does.

steps.title: Find your board
steps.description: The distribution board (or breaker box) is usually near your front door or in a utility area. It's a metal box on the wall.

steps.title: Open the panel
steps.description: Open the door of the box. You'll see rows of switches (called breakers). Each one controls power to a part of your home.

steps.title: Identify each breaker
steps.description: Turn off one breaker at a time and check which lights or outlets stop working. Write it down.

steps.title: Label everything
steps.description: Stick labels next to each breaker so you know which one is which. This saves time during emergencies.

steps.title: Know the main switch
steps.description: The biggest switch at the top cuts power to the whole house. Use it only in emergencies.

tips.proTip: Take a photo of your labeled board and save it on your phone for quick reference.

tips.title: Reset a Tripped Breaker
tips.subtitle: When the power goes out in one room, here's what to do.

steps.title: Go to your breaker box
steps.description: Open the distribution board. Look for a switch that is in the middle position — not fully on or off.

steps.title: Flip it off first
steps.description: Push that switch all the way to the OFF position.

steps.title: Then flip it on
steps.description: Now push it back to the ON position. You should hear a click.

steps.title: Check the room
steps.description: Go back and see if the power is restored. If it trips again, you may have too many things plugged in.

tips.proTip: If a breaker keeps tripping, unplug everything from that circuit and call an electrician.

tips.title: Replace a Fuse
tips.subtitle: Older homes use fuses. Here's how to swap one.

steps.title: Turn off the main switch
steps.description: Go to your fuse box and flip the main switch to OFF. Safety first!

steps.title: Find the blown fuse
steps.description: Look for a fuse that looks dark or has a broken wire inside. That's the one to replace.

steps.title: Remove the old fuse
steps.description: Unscrew or pull out the old fuse. Use insulated gloves just to be safe.

steps.title: Put in the new one
steps.description: Insert the new fuse. Make sure it has the same number (amps) as the old one.

steps.title: Turn the main back on
steps.description: Flip the main switch to ON, then check if the problem is fixed.

tips.proTip: Never use a fuse with a higher amp rating — it can cause a fire.

tips.title: Install a Dimmer Switch
tips.subtitle: Control how bright or soft your lights are.

steps.title: Turn off the breaker
steps.description: Switch off power to the light from your distribution board. Double-check with a wire tester.

steps.title: Remove the old switch
steps.description: Unscrew the faceplate and pull out the old switch. Note which wires go where.

steps.title: Connect the dimmer
steps.description: Attach the wires to the new dimmer following the instructions in the box. Match the wire colors.

steps.title: Mount and test
steps.description: Screw the dimmer into the wall box, attach the faceplate, and turn the breaker back on. Slide to test!

tips.proTip: Make sure your light bulbs are 'dimmable' — not all LEDs work with dimmers.

tips.title: Fix a Flickering Light
tips.subtitle: Stop that annoying flicker in seconds.

steps.title: Turn off the light
steps.description: Switch off the light and wait for the bulb to cool down a bit.

steps.title: Tighten the bulb
steps.description: Give the bulb a gentle clockwise twist. A loose bulb is the most common cause of flickering.

steps.title: Check the switch
steps.description: Flip the switch a few times. If it feels loose or wobbly, the switch might need replacing.

steps.title: Try a new bulb
steps.description: If tightening didn't help, swap in a new bulb. The old one might be going bad.

tips.proTip: If the whole room flickers, it could be a wiring issue — call a professional.

tips.title: Unclog a Drain
tips.subtitle: Clear a slow or blocked drain without harsh chemicals.

steps.title: Pour boiling water
steps.description: Boil a kettle and pour the hot water slowly down the drain. This loosens grease and soap buildup.

steps.title: Add baking soda
steps.description: Pour half a cup of baking soda into the drain. Let it sit for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Add vinegar
steps.description: Pour half a cup of vinegar down the drain. It will fizz — that's the cleaning action working!

steps.title: Wait and flush
steps.description: Wait 15 minutes then flush with more boiling water. Repeat if needed.

tips.proTip: Do this once a month to keep drains clear. Prevention is easier than fixing!

tips.title: Fix a Leaky Faucet
tips.subtitle: Stop that drip-drip-drip for good.

steps.title: Turn off the water
steps.description: Find the shut-off valve under the sink and turn it clockwise until it stops.

steps.title: Remove the handle
steps.description: Pry off the decorative cap, unscrew the handle screw, and pull the handle off.

steps.title: Replace the washer
steps.description: Inside you'll find a small rubber washer. Take it out and put in a new one of the same size.

steps.title: Put it back together
steps.description: Reassemble everything in reverse order. Turn the water back on and check — no more dripping!

tips.proTip: Bring the old washer to the hardware store so you get the exact right size.

tips.title: Shut Off the Main Water
tips.subtitle: Know this before a pipe bursts.
whatYouNeed: Nothing — just know where it is!

steps.title: Find the valve
steps.description: Look near your water meter, usually in the basement, garage, or outside near the street.

steps.title: Turn it off
steps.description: Turn the valve clockwise (righty-tighty) until it stops. If it's a lever, turn it so it's across the pipe.

steps.title: Test it
steps.description: Open a faucet inside. If no water comes out after a few seconds, you did it right.

tips.proTip: Show everyone in the house where this valve is. In an emergency, every second counts.

tips.title: Unclog a Toilet
tips.subtitle: Don't panic — this is fixable.

steps.title: Don't flush again
steps.description: If the water is rising, do NOT flush again. This will make it overflow.

steps.title: Place the plunger
steps.description: Put the plunger over the drain hole at the bottom of the bowl. Make sure it forms a seal.

steps.title: Plunge firmly
steps.description: Push down and pull up firmly about 15 times. Keep the seal tight.

steps.title: Check the flow
steps.description: Remove the plunger. If water drains, flush once to clear. If not, repeat.

tips.proTip: A flanged plunger (with an extra ring) works way better for toilets than a flat one.

tips.title: Fix a Running Toilet
tips.subtitle: Stop wasting water and money.

steps.title: Remove the tank lid
steps.description: Lift the lid off the back of the toilet. Set it somewhere safe — it's heavy and breakable.

steps.title: Check the flapper
steps.description: The flapper is the rubber piece at the bottom. If it's worn or doesn't sit flat, water leaks through.

steps.title: Replace the flapper
steps.description: Unhook the old flapper and snap the new one in place. They're cheap and easy to find.

steps.title: Adjust the float
steps.description: If the water level is too high, adjust the float arm or screw so water stops about an inch below the overflow tube.

tips.proTip: A running toilet can waste 200 gallons of water a day. Fix it fast!

tips.title: Prevent Frozen Pipes
tips.subtitle: Keep your pipes safe in cold weather.

steps.title: Insulate exposed pipes
steps.description: Wrap foam insulation around any pipes you can see in the basement, attic, or garage.

steps.title: Let faucets drip
steps.description: On very cold nights, let a thin stream of water run from faucets. Moving water doesn't freeze as easily.

steps.title: Keep the heat on
steps.description: Even if you're away, keep your home heated to at least 55°F (13°C). This protects your pipes.

tips.proTip: Open cabinet doors under sinks on cold nights to let warm air reach the pipes.

tips.title: Clean Kitchen Counters
tips.subtitle: A quick daily clean keeps germs away.
whatYouNeed: Spray bottle with soapy water or cleaner

steps.title: Clear the counter
steps.description: Move everything off the counter — jars, appliances, fruit bowls. You want to clean every inch.

steps.title: Spray it down
steps.description: Give the whole counter a good spray with your cleaner. Let it sit for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Wipe it clean
steps.description: Wipe from back to front, pushing crumbs off the edge into your hand or a dustpan.

steps.title: Dry it off
steps.description: Use a dry cloth to buff the counter. This prevents water spots and keeps it shiny.

tips.proTip: Mix equal parts water and white vinegar for a cheap, natural cleaner.

tips.title: Remove Carpet Stains
tips.subtitle: Act fast and the stain won't stand a chance.

steps.title: Blot, don't rub
steps.description: Press a clean cloth on the stain to soak up as much as possible. Rubbing pushes the stain deeper.

steps.title: Apply cleaning mix
steps.description: Mix a drop of dish soap with cold water. Dab it on the stain gently.

steps.title: Blot again
steps.description: Use a fresh part of the cloth to blot up the soapy water and stain. Repeat until the stain fades.

steps.title: Sprinkle baking soda
steps.description: Once dry, sprinkle baking soda on the spot. Let it sit for a few hours, then vacuum it up.

tips.proTip: Always use cold water on stains. Hot water can set many stains permanently.

tips.title: Clean Windows Streak-Free
tips.subtitle: Crystal clear windows in no time.

steps.title: Pick a cloudy day
steps.description: Direct sunlight dries the cleaner too fast, leaving streaks. Cloudy days are best.

steps.title: Spray the glass
steps.description: Spray the cleaner across the whole window. Cover it well.

steps.title: Wipe with squeegee
steps.description: Starting at the top, pull the squeegee down in one smooth stroke. Wipe the blade after each pass.

steps.title: Dry the edges
steps.description: Use a microfiber cloth to wipe any drips along the edges and corners.

tips.proTip: Newspaper works surprisingly well for a streak-free shine. Give it a try!

tips.title: Deep Clean Your Bathroom
tips.subtitle: Make your bathroom sparkle from top to bottom.

steps.title: Start with the toilet
steps.description: Squirt cleaner inside the bowl and let it sit while you clean other things.

steps.title: Spray surfaces
steps.description: Spray the sink, counter, and mirror with all-purpose cleaner.

steps.title: Scrub the shower/tub
steps.description: Spray and scrub the shower walls, tub, and faucets. Pay attention to corners where mold grows.

steps.title: Wipe everything down
steps.description: Wipe the mirror, sink, and counter. Scrub the toilet bowl with a brush, then wipe the outside.

steps.title: Mop the floor
steps.description: Sweep or vacuum first, then mop. Start from the far end and work toward the door.

tips.proTip: Clean your bathroom weekly. It's much faster when grime doesn't have time to build up.

tips.title: Dust Your Home Properly
tips.subtitle: Breathe easier with less dust around.

steps.title: Start high
steps.description: Dust ceiling fans, light fixtures, and the tops of shelves first. Dust falls down!

steps.title: Work your way down
steps.description: Wipe shelves, picture frames, TV screens, and furniture tops.

steps.title: Don't forget hidden spots
steps.description: Dust baseboards, window sills, and behind electronics.

steps.title: Finish with the floor
steps.description: Vacuum or sweep the floor last to catch all the dust that fell.

tips.proTip: Dampen your microfiber cloth slightly — it catches dust instead of spreading it around.

tips.title: Clean Your Washing Machine
tips.subtitle: Yes, even your washer needs a bath.

steps.title: Run an empty hot cycle
steps.description: Add 2 cups of white vinegar to the drum. Run it on the hottest setting with no clothes.

steps.title: Scrub the drum
steps.description: After the cycle, sprinkle baking soda inside and scrub with a damp cloth.

steps.title: Clean the door seal
steps.description: Wipe around the rubber door seal with vinegar. This is where mold likes to hide.

steps.title: Leave it open
steps.description: After cleaning, leave the door open to air dry. This prevents mold and bad smells.

tips.proTip: Do this once a month to keep your washer fresh and your clothes smelling great.

tips.title: Organize Your Pantry
tips.subtitle: Find what you need in seconds, not minutes.

steps.title: Take everything out
steps.description: Empty the whole pantry. Yes, everything. Check expiry dates as you go.

steps.title: Group similar items
steps.description: Put canned goods together, snacks together, baking supplies together. Like with like.

steps.title: Use clear containers
steps.description: Pour things like rice, pasta, and cereal into clear containers. You'll see when you're running low.

steps.title: Put everyday items at eye level
steps.description: Things you use daily should be easy to reach. Rarely used items go up high.

tips.proTip: A Lazy Susan in the corner makes reaching things in deep shelves much easier.

tips.title: Deep Clean an Oven
tips.subtitle: No more smoke every time you bake.

steps.title: Make a paste
steps.description: Mix half a cup of baking soda with a few tablespoons of water to make a thick paste.

steps.title: Coat the inside
steps.description: Spread the paste all over the inside of the oven. Avoid the heating elements. It will turn brown.

steps.title: Wait overnight
steps.description: Let the paste sit for at least 12 hours. It breaks down all the baked-on grease.

steps.title: Spray with vinegar
steps.description: Spray vinegar over the paste. It will fizz. This loosens everything up.

steps.title: Wipe it all out
steps.description: Use a damp cloth to wipe away the paste and grime. Repeat until the oven is clean.

tips.proTip: Place a sheet of foil at the bottom of your oven to catch drips and make future cleaning easier.

tips.title: Maintain Your Refrigerator
tips.subtitle: Keep food fresh and energy bills low.

steps.title: Check the temperature
steps.description: Your fridge should be at 37°F (3°C) and the freezer at 0°F (-18°C). Adjust if needed.

steps.title: Clean the shelves
steps.description: Remove food, take out shelves, and wash them with warm soapy water.

steps.title: Wipe the inside
steps.description: Wipe down the walls and door seals with a damp cloth.

steps.title: Deodorize
steps.description: Place an open box of baking soda inside. It absorbs bad smells for up to 3 months.

tips.proTip: Clean the coils behind or under your fridge twice a year — it saves energy.

tips.title: Sharpen Kitchen Knives
tips.subtitle: A sharp knife is safer than a dull one.

steps.title: Hold the rod steady
steps.description: Place the tip of the honing rod on a cutting board. Hold it straight up.

steps.title: Angle the knife
steps.description: Hold the knife at about a 20-degree angle against the rod.

steps.title: Slide down and across
steps.description: Pull the knife down the rod from heel to tip, as if you're slicing a thin piece off.

steps.title: Alternate sides
steps.description: Do this 5 times on each side. Test the sharpness on a tomato.

tips.proTip: Hone your knives every time you cook. Sharpen with a stone only a few times a year.

tips.title: Prevent Kitchen Fires
tips.subtitle: Simple steps to keep cooking safe.

steps.title: Never leave cooking unattended
steps.description: Stay in the kitchen when frying or grilling. If you must leave, turn off the stove.

steps.title: Keep flammables away
steps.description: Move towels, paper, and curtains away from the stove.

steps.title: Know how to smother a grease fire
steps.description: Never use water on a grease fire. Slide a lid over the pan and turn off the heat.

steps.title: Keep an extinguisher nearby
steps.description: Have a fire extinguisher in the kitchen. Check it yearly.

tips.proTip: Baking soda can also smother a small grease fire. Keep a box near the stove.

tips.title: Get Rid of Kitchen Odors
tips.subtitle: Your kitchen should smell like dinner, not last week's dinner.

steps.title: Simmer citrus
steps.description: Slice a lemon and simmer it in a pot of water on the stove. It freshens up the whole kitchen.

steps.title: Deodorize the sink
steps.description: Pour baking soda down the drain, followed by vinegar. Let it fizz, then rinse with hot water.

steps.title: Clean the trash can
steps.description: Wash the inside of your trash can with soapy water. Sprinkle baking soda at the bottom before adding a new bag.

tips.proTip: Grind lemon peels in the garbage disposal to keep it smelling fresh.

tips.title: Patch a Hole in the Wall
tips.subtitle: Fix nail holes and small dents like a pro.

steps.title: Clean the hole
steps.description: Remove any loose bits around the hole. Brush away dust.

steps.title: Apply spackling paste
steps.description: Scoop a small amount of paste onto your putty knife. Press it into the hole and smooth it flat.

steps.title: Let it dry
steps.description: Wait for the paste to dry completely. This usually takes 1-2 hours.

steps.title: Sand and paint
steps.description: Sand the patch smooth with fine sandpaper. Then touch it up with matching paint.

tips.proTip: Keep a small jar of your wall paint color handy for quick touch-ups.

tips.title: Tighten a Loose Door Handle
tips.subtitle: No more wobbly handles.

steps.title: Look for screws
steps.description: Check the base of the handle for visible screws.

steps.title: Find hidden screws
steps.description: If you don't see screws, look for a small slot on the side. Insert a flathead screwdriver to pop off the cover.

steps.title: Tighten them up
steps.description: Use your screwdriver to tighten all the screws. Turn clockwise until snug.

steps.title: Test the handle
steps.description: Turn it a few times. It should feel firm and solid.

tips.proTip: A tiny dab of thread-locking glue on the screws keeps them from loosening again.

tips.title: Fix a Squeaky Door
tips.subtitle: Silence that annoying squeak in seconds.

steps.title: Find the noisy hinge
steps.description: Open and close the door slowly to figure out which hinge is squeaking.

steps.title: Apply lubricant
steps.description: Spray a little WD-40 on the hinge pin, or drip a few drops of cooking oil on it.

steps.title: Move the door
steps.description: Open and close the door several times to work the oil into the hinge.

steps.title: Wipe excess
steps.description: Clean up any drips with a paper towel so it doesn't stain the floor.

tips.proTip: Petroleum jelly works great too and lasts longer than oil.

tips.title: Fix a Squeaky Floor
tips.subtitle: Walk without waking up the whole house.

steps.title: Find the squeak
steps.description: Walk around slowly and mark the squeaky spots with tape.

steps.title: Try powder first
steps.description: Sprinkle talcum powder or powdered graphite between the boards. Work it in with your foot.

steps.title: Screw from below
steps.description: If you can get under the floor, drive a short screw up through the subfloor into the squeaky board.

steps.title: Test it out
steps.description: Walk over the area again. The squeak should be gone or much quieter.

tips.proTip: Baby powder works just as well as talcum powder for a quick fix.

tips.title: Seal a Drafty Window
tips.subtitle: Stop cold air from sneaking in.

steps.title: Find the drafts
steps.description: Hold a lit candle near window edges on a windy day. If the flame flickers, there's a draft.

steps.title: Apply weatherstripping
steps.description: Stick self-adhesive weatherstripping along the window frame where it opens and closes.

steps.title: Caulk the gaps
steps.description: For cracks between the window frame and wall, apply a thin bead of caulk. Smooth with a wet finger.

steps.title: Check your work
steps.description: Do the candle test again. The flame should stay steady this time.

tips.proTip: Draft-proofing your windows can cut heating bills by up to 25%.

tips.title: Hang a Picture Straight
tips.subtitle: No more crooked frames on your walls.

steps.title: Choose the spot
steps.description: The center of the picture should be at eye level — about 57 inches from the floor.

steps.title: Mark and nail
steps.description: Make a small pencil mark. Hammer in a nail or picture hook at a slight upward angle.

steps.title: Hang and level
steps.description: Hang the picture and use a level (or the level app on your phone) to make sure it's straight.

steps.title: Add bumpers
steps.description: Stick small felt or rubber bumpers to the bottom corners. This keeps it from sliding and protects the wall.

tips.proTip: Use painters tape to mark the spot first — you can reposition without making extra holes.

tips.title: Water Plants Properly
tips.subtitle: Not too much, not too little — just right.

steps.title: Check the soil
steps.description: Stick your finger about an inch into the soil. If it's dry, it's time to water.

steps.title: Water at the base
steps.description: Pour water at the base of the plant, not on the leaves. Wet leaves can get moldy.

steps.title: Water deeply
steps.description: Give it a good, slow soak rather than a light sprinkle. This encourages deeper roots.

steps.title: Water in the morning
steps.description: Morning is the best time to water. The plant has all day to absorb it before the evening cool.

tips.proTip: Most houseplants die from overwatering, not under-watering. When in doubt, wait a day.

tips.title: Mow a Lawn Evenly
tips.subtitle: Get that carpet-smooth look.

steps.title: Set the right height
steps.description: Never cut more than one-third of the grass height at once. Set your mower blade higher.

steps.title: Mow in straight lines
steps.description: Pick one direction and mow in straight rows. Slightly overlap each pass.

steps.title: Alternate your pattern
steps.description: Next time you mow, go in a different direction. This keeps the grass standing up straight.

steps.title: Leave the clippings
steps.description: Short clippings break down and feed the lawn. Only bag them if they're clumpy.

tips.proTip: Mow when the grass is dry. Wet grass clumps and gives an uneven cut.

tips.title: Clean Gutters Safely
tips.subtitle: Prevent water damage with a quick cleanup.

steps.title: Set up your ladder safely
steps.description: Place the ladder on firm, level ground. Have someone hold it if possible. Never lean too far.

steps.title: Scoop out debris
steps.description: Using gloves and a trowel, scoop out leaves, twigs, and muck. Drop it into your bucket.

steps.title: Flush with water
steps.description: Use a hose to flush out remaining dirt. Check that water flows freely through the downspout.

steps.title: Check for damage
steps.description: Look for cracks, sagging, or loose screws. Fix any small issues now before they get worse.

tips.proTip: Clean gutters twice a year — spring and fall. Install gutter guards to reduce buildup.

tips.title: Start a Small Garden
tips.subtitle: Grow your own herbs and veggies, even in small spaces.

steps.title: Choose easy plants
steps.description: Start with herbs like basil and mint, or veggies like tomatoes and lettuce. They're hard to mess up.

steps.title: Pick a sunny spot
steps.description: Most food plants need 6 hours of sunlight a day. A windowsill or balcony works.

steps.title: Fill pots with soil
steps.description: Use good potting soil. Fill your containers leaving about an inch from the top.

steps.title: Plant and water
steps.description: Plant seeds at the depth shown on the packet, or transplant seedlings. Water gently.

tips.proTip: Herbs like basil and cilantro grow fast and taste way better fresh from your own garden.

tips.title: Maintain Outdoor Furniture
tips.subtitle: Keep your patio set looking like new.

steps.title: Wipe down weekly
steps.description: A quick wipe with a damp cloth each week prevents dirt buildup.

steps.title: Deep clean monthly
steps.description: Scrub with warm soapy water and a brush. Rinse well and let dry completely.

steps.title: Apply protector
steps.description: Spray a UV-protective coat on wood or plastic furniture to prevent fading.

steps.title: Cover or store
steps.description: When not in use for long periods, cover furniture or move it to a sheltered area.

tips.proTip: Bring cushions inside during rain. They take forever to dry and can get moldy.

tips.title: Remove Weeds Effectively
tips.subtitle: Kick weeds out before they take over.

steps.title: Pull after rain
steps.description: Weeds come out much easier when the soil is moist. Wait until after a rain.

steps.title: Grab low and pull straight
steps.description: Grip the weed as close to the ground as you can. Pull straight up to get the root out.

steps.title: Use a tool for tough ones
steps.description: For weeds with deep roots, slide a weeding fork under the root and pry it up.

steps.title: Mulch to prevent regrowth
steps.description: Spread 2-3 inches of mulch over bare soil. It blocks sunlight and stops new weeds from growing.

tips.proTip: Regular weeding is easier than a big cleanup. Spend 5 minutes every few days.

tips.title: Sort Clothes for Washing
tips.subtitle: The first step to laundry done right.

steps.title: Separate by color
steps.description: Put whites in one pile, darks in another, and bright colors in a third. This prevents colors from bleeding.

steps.title: Check labels
steps.description: Look at the care labels inside clothes. Some items need cold water or gentle cycles.

steps.title: Separate heavy and light
steps.description: Wash heavy items like jeans and towelsparts from delicate items like blouses and underwear.

steps.title: Empty pockets
steps.description: Check every single pocket. Tissues, coins, and pens can ruin a whole load.

tips.proTip: Get three laundry bags — whites, darks, colors. Sort as you go and laundry day is half done.

tips.title: Remove Tough Stains
tips.subtitle: Don't throw it away — try this first.

steps.title: Act fast
steps.description: The sooner you treat a stain, the easier it is to remove. Don't let it dry.

steps.title: Rinse with cold water
steps.description: Run cold water through the back of the stain to push it out of the fabric.

steps.title: Apply stain remover
steps.description: Dab stain remover or a drop of dish soap on the spot. Gently work it in with a toothbrush.

steps.title: Soak and wash
steps.description: Let it soak for 15 minutes, then wash as normal. Check the stain before drying — heat sets stains.

tips.proTip: For blood stains, always use cold water. Hot water cooks the proteins and makes it permanent.

tips.title: Iron Clothes Properly
tips.subtitle: Look crisp and polished in minutes.

steps.title: Set the right temperature
steps.description: Check the label. Low heat for synthetics, medium for wool, high for cotton and linen.

steps.title: Iron inside out
steps.description: Turn dark clothes inside out to prevent shiny marks.

steps.title: Use smooth strokes
steps.description: Press and slide the iron in long, smooth strokes. Don't push too hard or leave it in one spot.

steps.title: Hang immediately
steps.description: Put the ironed item on a hanger right away. If you fold it, wrinkles come right back.

tips.proTip: Iron slightly damp clothes — it's easier and gives better results.

tips.title: Wash Delicates Safely
tips.subtitle: Keep your favorite clothes lasting longer.

steps.title: Use a mesh bag
steps.description: Put delicates in a mesh bag before tossing them in the machine. It protects them from stretching.

steps.title: Use cold water
steps.description: Always wash delicates in cold water on a gentle cycle. Hot water can shrink or damage them.

steps.title: Skip the dryer
steps.description: Lay delicates flat on a towel to dry, or hang them. The dryer's heat is too harsh.

tips.proTip: For items labeled 'hand wash only,' fill a basin with cool water and mild soap. Soak, gently swirl, and rinse.

tips.title: Dry Clothes Faster
tips.subtitle: Speed up drying when you're in a rush.

steps.title: Use a dry towel trick
steps.description: Throw a clean, dry towel into the dryer with wet clothes. It absorbs moisture and speeds things up.

steps.title: Don't overload
steps.description: Clothes need room to tumble. A half-full dryer dries faster than a stuffed one.

steps.title: Clean the lint trap
steps.description: A clogged lint trap slows drying. Clean it before every load.

steps.title: Spin twice if air drying
steps.description: If hanging to dry, run an extra spin cycle to wring out more water first.

tips.proTip: Hanging clothes in a breezy spot with good airflow dries them faster than a still room.

tips.title: Fold Clothes Neatly
tips.subtitle: Save space and find things faster.

steps.title: Fold on a flat surface
steps.description: Use a table or bed. Smooth out the item before you start.

steps.title: T-shirts: thirds then half
steps.description: Fold sleeves in toward the center, then fold the bottom up to the collar.

steps.title: Pants: halves
steps.description: Fold one leg on top of the other, then fold in half or thirds depending on drawer size.

steps.title: Stand items vertically
steps.description: Store folded items standing up in drawers like files. You'll see everything at a glance.

tips.proTip: The KonMari method of vertical folding saves up to 50% more drawer space.

tips.title: Test Smoke Detectors
tips.subtitle: A working alarm can save your life.

steps.title: Find all detectors
steps.description: Walk through every room. You should have one in each bedroom, hallway, and the kitchen.

steps.title: Press the test button
steps.description: Hold the test button for 3-5 seconds. You should hear a loud beep.

steps.title: Replace batteries
steps.description: If the beep is weak or doesn't happen, replace the battery. This is usually a 9V battery.

steps.title: Replace old units
steps.description: Smoke detectors expire after 10 years. Check the date on the back and replace if needed.

tips.proTip: Test your smoke detectors every month. Change batteries every 6 months.

tips.title: Use a Fire Extinguisher
tips.subtitle: Remember: PASS — Pull, Aim, Squeeze, Sweep.

steps.title: Pull the pin
steps.description: Pull out the small metal pin at the top. This unlocks the extinguisher.

steps.title: Aim at the base
steps.description: Point the nozzle at the BASE of the fire — not the flames at the top.

steps.title: Squeeze the handle
steps.description: Squeeze the handle firmly to release the spray.

steps.title: Sweep side to side
steps.description: Move the nozzle slowly from side to side across the base of the fire until it's out.

tips.proTip: Stand 6 to 8 feet away from the fire. If it doesn't go out quickly, get out and call 911.

tips.title: Childproof Your Home
tips.subtitle: Little hands can get into big trouble.

steps.title: Cover electrical outlets
steps.description: Plug outlet covers into every open outlet, especially low ones that kids can reach.

steps.title: Lock cabinets
steps.description: Install child locks on cabinets with cleaning supplies, medicines, or sharp objects.

steps.title: Pad sharp corners
steps.description: Put soft corner protectors on coffee tables, counter edges, and furniture corners.

steps.title: Install safety gates
steps.description: Place gates at the top and bottom of stairs and at doorways to rooms with hazards.

steps.title: Secure heavy furniture
steps.description: Anchor bookshelves, dressers, and TVs to the wall so they can't tip over.

tips.proTip: Get down on your hands and knees and look around from a child's eye level. You'll spot dangers you never noticed.

tips.title: Create a Home Emergency Kit
tips.subtitle: Be ready for the unexpected.

steps.title: Gather water & food
steps.description: Store 1 gallon of water per person per day for 3 days. Add canned foods and a can opener.

steps.title: Add first aid supplies
steps.description: Include bandages, antiseptic, pain relievers, prescription medications, and any allergy meds.

steps.title: Include tools & light
steps.description: Add a flashlight, extra batteries, a whistle, and a basic multi-tool.

steps.title: Add personal documents
steps.description: Keep copies of IDs, insurance papers, and emergency contacts in a waterproof bag.

tips.proTip: Check and refresh your emergency kit every 6 months. Rotate food and water.

tips.title: Prevent Carbon Monoxide Poisoning
tips.subtitle: The silent danger you can't see or smell.

steps.title: Install CO detectors
steps.description: Place a carbon monoxide detector on every floor and near bedrooms. They look like smoke detectors.

steps.title: Test regularly
steps.description: Press the test button monthly, just like smoke detectors.

steps.title: Never run engines indoors
steps.description: Never run a car, generator, or grill inside the house or garage — even with the door open.

steps.title: Service heating systems
steps.description: Have your furnace, water heater, and chimney inspected every year.

tips.proTip: If your CO alarm goes off, get everyone outside immediately and call 911.

tips.title: Make a Family Safety Plan
tips.subtitle: When everyone knows the plan, everyone stays safe.

steps.title: Choose a meeting point
steps.description: Pick a spot outside your home where everyone meets if you have to leave in an emergency.

steps.title: Plan two escape routes
steps.description: From every room, plan two ways to get out. Practice walking them with the whole family.

steps.title: Share emergency contacts
steps.description: Make sure every family member knows important phone numbers — or has them saved on their phone.

steps.title: Practice regularly
steps.description: Do a fire drill every few months. Talk about what to do during storms, earthquakes, or power outages.

tips.proTip: Teach young kids their full name, address, and how to call for help.
